summary: n8n-nodes-devops-utils exfiltrates host recon and opens reverse shell to 103.27.109.184:8892 on load
details: The package masquerades as an n8n community-node utility (package.json declares n8n.nodes and the n8n-community-node-package keyword; MyNode.js is an empty stub returning [[]]), but index.js runs a self-invoking function at module load that (1) harvests host reconnaissance — hostname, machine-id, OS release, network interfaces and routes, /etc/hosts, listening sockets, mounts, cgroup, process list, home and /tmp listings, and a scan of the Docker gateway range 172.17.0.1-10 — (2) shells out to `env | grep -iE "api|key|token|secret|pass|db|mongo|redis|evolution|whatsapp|webhook|n8n|docker|container"` to scrape credential-shaped environment variables, and TCP-sends the combined blob to 103.27.109.184:8892. The same load-time code path spawns `/bin/bash -i` and pipes its stdio over a second TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8892, giving the remote party interactive shell access on the installer's host. Both behaviors fire automatically when n8n loads the community node.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
